jd_text: Software engineer position at a tech company. Requires Python and React.
report:
Running session checks and reading the evaluation mode so the report follows career-ops blocks A–G.
Running session checks and reading the evaluation mode so the report follows career-ops blocks A–G.
## A) Role Summary

| Field | Assessment |
|--------|------------|
| **Archetype** | General software engineering (no clean AI archetype fit). Nearest hybrid: **product/full-stack engineer** with frontend lean — not FDE, LLMOps, or Agentic as defined in career-ops. |
| **Domain** | Unknown — JD gives no product, industry, or stack beyond two languages. |
| **Function** | Build (inferred only; not stated). |
| **Seniority** | Unspecified — could be mid or senior. |
| **Remote** | Unspecified. |
| **Team size** | Unspecified. |
| **TL;DR** | A placeholder posting for a generic “software engineer” who knows **Python** and **React**, with no company, scope, level, or location — impossible to assess fit beyond bare skill overlap. |

**Liveness note:** JD was pasted as text only (no URL). Posting freshness and apply-path status cannot be verified.

---

## B) Match with CV

| JD requirement | CV evidence | Match |
|----------------|-------------|-------|
| **React** | Skills: “TypeScript, **React 19**, … Next.js”; Spotshop PWA: “**React 19 + Vite + Tailwind CSS**”; Plugly AI Chat widget (2025–2026) | **Strong** — multiple production and in-development React deliverables. |
| **Python** | Skills: “**Python (FastAPI)**”; Career Ops Dashboard: “**FastAPI backend**”; side infra on Linux VPS | **Partial** — real Python/FastAPI on side projects; Bewise production work is primarily frontend + DanDomain/Shopify, with Spotshop backend described as “**C# REST backend**” (not Python). |
| **Software engineer** (implicit full-stack) | Career Ops Dashboard (Astro + FastAPI + nginx deploy); autonomous agent infra; Bewise widgets + PWAs | **Moderate** — full-stack capability demonstrated on own projects; day job is frontend-heavy with separate backend owners. |
| **E-commerce** (not in JD) | 8+ years DanDomain/Shopify; “**500K+ product catalogs**”; widgets “**across multiple live production webshops**” | **N/A to JD** — strong differentiator if role is product/e-commerce, invisible if role is generic internal tools. |
| **AI** (not in JD) | Plugly AI Chat; streaming LLM UIs; Career Ops “**Real AI evaluation via multi-provider routing**” | **N/A to JD** — aligns with your stated direction (“**AI-native frontend is my direction**”), not requested here. |

### Gaps

| Gap | Blocker? | Mitigation |
|-----|----------|------------|
| **Python depth vs React** | Soft — only if role is Python-primary backend | Lead with React production wins; frame Python via Career Ops Dashboard + FastAPI + agent infra as “I own the full vertical when the team is small.” |
| **Seniority unknown** | Soft | Default to senior framing: 8+ years, multi-shop production, driver PWA shipped — ask early whether they want IC senior or mid. |
| **No company/domain** | Hard for decision-making | Do not invest application effort until company, team, and scope are known. |
| **Title mismatch** (AI Frontend Engineer vs generic SWE) | Soft | Reframe summary to “frontend engineer with production React + Python for AI-backed products” rather than pure backend SWE. |

---

## C) Level & Strategy

**Level detected:** Indeterminate. “Software engineer” + Python/React spans mid-level full-stack through senior frontend-with-backend-skills.

**Your natural level for this shape of role:** **Senior frontend / mid–senior full-stack** — 8+ years production e-commerce UIs, React 19 shipped (Spotshop PWA), agency delivery under real constraints.

### Sell senior without overstating

- **Production React at scale:** “Built and shipped **Spotshop Transporter PWA** — React 19 + Vite — integrated with order APIs for pickup/delivery workflows.”
- **Platform scar tissue:** “**5+ years DanDomain Hostedshop** — JSON API, 500K+ catalogs, pricing edge cases — not tutorial React.”
- **Python where it counts:** “**Career Ops Dashboard**: Astro frontend, **FastAPI backend**, deployed on my own nginx + Tailwind stack.”
- **AI as accelerator, not fiction:** Plugly + streaming LLM work — only if the team cares about AI product surfaces.

### If they downlevel

- Accept only if comp reflects senior frontend (see D) and Python is adjunct, not 70% of the role.
- Negotiate 6-month scope review: “If I’m owning React architecture + one Python service, title/level should reflect that.”

---

## D) Comp & Demand

| Item | Finding |
|------|---------|
| **Role-specific comp** | **No data** — company unknown; JD has no salary band or location. |
| **Market context (qualitative)** | Python + React full-stack roles remain common in EU tech; demand is steady but not niche-scarce. |
| **Your positioning** | Denmark-based; profile does not state a salary target in the pasted CV. Without company tier (startup vs enterprise), any number would be invented — **withheld**. |
| **Demand for your profile** | Stronger market fit for **“React + e-commerce + AI UI”** than for generic “Python + React” — this JD underspecifies where your edge pays off. |

**Action:** Before comp talk, require: location/remote policy, level, and whether Python is primary backend or supporting tooling.

---

## E) Customization Plan

| # | Section | Current | Proposed change | Why |
|---|---------|---------|-----------------|-----|
| 1 | **Headline** | AI Frontend Engineer | **Senior Frontend Engineer · React · Python (FastAPI)** — or keep AI if company is product/AI | JD keywords are generic; mirror “software engineer” + stack literally for ATS. |
| 2 | **Summary** | AI-native + e-commerce lead | Add one line: “**Production React 19** (driver PWA, shop widgets); **Python/FastAPI** on full-stack side projects.” | Directly maps the two JD bullets. |
| 3 | **Skills order** | Frontend first | Put **React 19**, **TypeScript**, **Python (FastAPI)** in first line of skills block | ATS scan order. |
| 4 | **Experience — Bewise** | Widget-heavy narrative | Add explicit bullet: “**Spotshop PWA** — sole frontend owner, React 19, API integration with order/payment flows.” | Proves “software engineer” beyond widgets. |
| 5 | **Side projects** | Career Ops mentioned | One bullet: “**FastAPI** REST API + multi-page Astro app; self-hosted deploy” | Closes Python gap for recruiters skimming. |

### Top 5 CV changes
1. Mirror **“Python”** and **“React”** in summary (exact JD terms).
2. Elevate **Spotshop PWA** above fold — strongest “engineer” story.
3. Single **FastAPI** bullet under Career Ops with deploy detail.
4. De-emphasize DanDomain jargon unless JD hints e-commerce.
5. Keep AI bullets secondary unless you learn the role is AI product.

### Top 5 LinkedIn changes
1. Headline: `Senior Frontend Engineer | React · TypeScript · Python (FastAPI) | E-commerce & AI UIs`
2. Featured: Spotshop PWA + Career Ops Dashboard links/screenshots.
3. Skills endorsements order: React, TypeScript, Python, FastAPI, Next.js.
4. About: first sentence = years + React production; second = Python on full-stack projects.
5. Remove or soften claims not in CV (profile.yml mentions gaming/blockchain paths not in pasted CV — keep LinkedIn consistent with `cv.md`).

---

## F) Interview Plan

| # | JD requirement | STAR+R story | S | T | A | R | Reflection |
|---|----------------|--------------|---|---|---|---|------------|
| 1 | **React** | Spotshop Transporter PWA | Driver app needed for pickup/delivery | Own frontend end-to-end | React 19 + Vite + Tailwind; integrated DanDomain order API via C# backend | Shipped driver-facing PWA | Learned to design for mobile field use and tight API contracts with a separate backend dev. |
| 2 | **React** (scale/complexity) | Bewise Plus widgets | Multiple SMB shops, shared widget suite | Maintain widgets across live stores | Smarty + JsRender, DanDomain JSON API, catalog/pricing edge cases | Widgets live on **multiple production webshops** | Platform quirks (500K+ catalogs, API failures) matter as much as component design. |
| 3 | **Python** | Career Ops Dashboard | Personal job-search automation | Full-stack tool, self-hosted | Astro UI + **FastAPI** backend, nginx + Tailscale deploy | Functional multi-page dashboard with real AI routing | Python role was “enough to ship” — good for full-stack IC, not ML backend lead. |
| 4 | **Python + product** | Plugly AI Chat | Shops need shop-aware AI assistant | Shop-agnostic widget, zero per-store config | DOM context, `/json/products`, Cloudflare Worker → OpenRouter | Dev-stage assistant across DanDomain shops | Multi-model routing belongs in UI architecture conversation if role is product-facing. |
| 5 | **Software engineer / delivery** | Shopify theme (Njord Sølv) | Client theme + Storefront API | Custom theme from scratch | Liquid + Tailwind, OAuth, Storefront API | Delivered custom storefront | Shows you can jump stacks when the product demands it. |
| 6 | **Ownership** | Autonomous agent infra | Persistent agent on VPS | Run without managed PaaS | systemd, Docker, nginx, multi-model routing | Running personal agent + knowledge vault | Demonstrates ops maturity; use only if they value self-hosted/full-stack ownership. |

**Recommended case study:** **Spotshop PWA** — clearest “software engineer” narrative: mobile React app, real users (drivers), API integration, shipped.

**Red-flag questions**

| Question | Answer angle |
|----------|----------------|
| “Your title is frontend — can you do Python backend?” | Honest split: primary strength is React production; Python proven on **Career Ops (FastAPI)** and automation — not 5 years Django at scale. |
| “Why leave agency work?” | (Only if true for you — not in CV.) Otherwise: focus on wanting product depth / AI-native product surfaces. |
| “Experience with our stack?” | Ask what stack is — JD only names two languages. Don’t invent familiarity. |

---

## G) Posting Legitimacy

**Assessment tier:** **Proceed with Caution**

| Signal | Finding | Weight |
|--------|---------|--------|
| Posting freshness | Not verifiable (text only, no URL) | Neutral |
| Apply path | Unknown | Neutral |
| Description quality | **Very low** — no company name, team, seniority, location, comp, or scope; only “Python and React” | **Concerning** |
| Requirements realism | Minimal requirements; no contradictions, but also no substance | Neutral |
| Company hiring signals | Company **unknown** — cannot search layoffs/hiring | Neutral |
| Reposting | Cannot check | Neutral |
| Role–company fit | Cannot assess | Neutral |

**Context notes:** This reads like a **stub or recruiter teaser**, not a complete posting. Generic two-stack JDs correlate with mass outreach or unfinished reqs — but they can also be legitimate first contact before a real JD. **Do not treat this as a vetted opportunity** until you have: company name, live posting URL, team description, level, and location/remote policy.

---

**Score:** **3.2/5**

| Dimension | Rationale |
|-----------|-----------|
| CV match | React: strong; Python: demonstrated but secondary |
| North Star | Sideways vs **AI Frontend Engineer** positioning |
| Comp / culture | Unknown |
| Red flags | No company, no URL, ultra-thin JD |

**Legitimacy:** **Proceed with Caution**

---

**Recommendation:** Skill overlap is real on **React** and plausible on **Python**, but this is not evaluation-ready as an application target. Ask for the full JD, company name, and URL; re-run evaluation then. If the real role is **backend-heavy Python**, score likely drops toward **2.5–3.0** unless they want frontend-led full-stack.
